title: Canonical Parity Economics And Posture Source

taxonomy.label: verified
taxonomy.definition: Code path plus current test or local operating evidence.

taxonomy.label: partial
taxonomy.definition: Code path exists with explicit scope limits or measured-but-narrow evidence.

taxonomy.label: experimental
taxonomy.definition: Present in code/config but not ready for parity or economics marketing claims.

parity_posture: Do not claim exact UX parity or exact feature parity with native Agent Teams.
Do not publish single-number parity percentages.
Use only evidence-labeled capability claims using the canonical taxonomy.
Hybrid/native execution paths remain experimental until current end-to-end evidence exists.
native_advantages: In-process teammate lifecycle semantics in a single runtime.
Integrated native UI and runtime linkage without external coordinator polling.

in_process_display.label: partial
in_process_display.description: coord_watch_output provides functional output monitoring via MCP tool call. Not identical UX to native Shift+Down keypress (tool call vs keypress). Code path verified at lib/workers.js:1101. No dedicated test coverage for coord_watch_output — classified partial, not verified.

lead_advantages: Pre-edit conflict detection and conflict lifecycle visibility across active sessions.
Operator-grade dashboard and API orchestration for multi-terminal workflows.
Filesystem coordination path with zero API-token coordination overhead.
Policy and governance controls around worker execution (budget/spawn/approval/checkpoint).
economics_posture: Do not claim universal savings or blanket cheaper-than-native outcomes.
Filesystem coordination can claim zero API-token coordination overhead on that path.
Throughput and economics claims beyond that path must stay evidence-scoped to the workflow under discussion.

economics_verdicts.label: verified
economics_verdicts.scope: Claim allowed for coordination traffic on the filesystem path only; excludes worker/model token usage.

economics_verdicts.label: partial
economics_verdicts.scope: Claim allowed only when tied to named measured artifacts and explicit workflow boundaries.

economics_verdicts.claim: Universal cheaper-than-native or universal savings claim
economics_verdicts.label: experimental
economics_verdicts.scope: Not claimable without production-measured, workflow-matched evidence across scenarios.

release_blocker_posture: Release blockers are failing release-quality gates, not unresolved parity/economics ambitions.
Parity and economics gaps remain posture limits until promoted by fresh evidence.

forbidden_claim_patterns.pattern: Replicate Claude Code's native Agent Teams exactly
Everything Agent Teams can do, the Lead System can do too
